action: The R9.2 triage capacity clamp (internal/triagecap) systematically OVERCOUNTS committed coverage floors because its known-package vocabulary collides with the triage bullet contract's mandatory metadata fields. CountCommittedFloors (floors.go:59) counts one floor per distinct known package whose name appears as a whole token in a floor-bearing ## top_n bullet — but KnownPackages collects directory BASENAMES under go/internal and go/cmd, which include `evidence` (go/internal/core/evidence) and `scout` (go/internal/phases/scout). Every contract-conformant bullet ends with `evidence=..., source=scout`, so every floor-bearing bullet gets +2 phantom floors. Observed live in cycle 301 (soak #2): triage committed exactly 2 coverage bullets (clihealth=1 pkg, ledger=1 pkg → true count 2) but the gate counted (1+2)+(1+2)=6 > cap 5 (K=4) and BLOCKed; both correction-ladder retries re-emitted contract-conformant reports and deterministically re-counted 6 — an unconvergeable correction loop, because the agent cannot remove the contract-mandated tokens. Burned correction 2/2 and failed the triage phase. ANY future cycle whose triage commits >=2 coverage bullets hits the same wall at K=4. Fix direction (pick one, with TDD pin reproducing the cycle-301 report): (a) strip `key=value` metadata fields (evidence=, source=, priority=, defer_reason=) from the bullet text before tokenizing in mentionedPackages, (b) blocklist contract-vocabulary tokens (evidence, scout, source) from the candidate set, or (c) match packages only as path-qualified mentions (go/internal/<pkg> or <pkg>/file.go), which is the strongest fix since bare basenames also collide with prose words (e.g. `core`, `config`, `policy` as English words). Acceptance: cycle-301's verbatim 2-bullet triage report counts 2, an actually-overpacked report still counts correctly, and a bullet naming a package ONLY in evidence= path still counts that package if path-qualified matching is chosen. ADDITIONAL FINDING (window poisoning): the throughput window state.json:triageThroughput is ITSELF phantom-inflated — its single entry {cycle:298, floors:4} came from cycle 298's one floor-bearing bullet counting 4 (1 real `gc` + 3 phantoms: `evidence` and `scout` from the contract fields, plus `paths` matching the PROSE phrase 'error paths' — go/internal/core/paths or similar basename). True observed throughput was 1 floor. So K=4 and cap=5 are both fabricated numbers. The fix must ALSO rebaseline or clear triageThroughput after correcting the counter, otherwise the healed counter will be judged against a poisoned K. Cycles 299/300 passed the gate only because their bullets cited no percentage targets (not floor-bearing, counted 0) — the bug was latent until triage wrote precise coverage evidence in 301.
evidence_pointer: go/internal/triagecap/floors.go:59 (CountCommittedFloors), floors.go:119 (KnownPackages basenames), .evolve/runs/cycle-301/triage-report.md (2 bullets counted as 6), evolve-batch-w4soak2-20260612.log triage-cap BLOCK x3 (initial + correction 1/2 + 2/2), go/internal/core/evidence + go/internal/phases/scout (the colliding basenames); .evolve/state.json triageThroughput=[{cycle:298,floors:4}]; .evolve/operator-salvage/cycle-301-triagecap-phantom-floors/ (full investigation snapshot)
